rules: Turn off, disconnect power, and discharge electrode to ground before setting down torch/electrode holder and before service
Do not touch energized electrical parts
Wear dry, insulating gloves — do not wear wet or damaged gloves
Connect to grounded, GFCI-protected power supply only
Do not use near water or damp objects
People with pacemakers must consult physician before use
Insulate yourself from workpiece and ground — use dry rubber mats, plywood, or dry insulating material
Wear ANSI-approved welding eye protection with at least shade number 10 lens
Wear leather leggings, fire resistant shoes or boots
Do not wear pants with cuffs or shirts with open pockets
Wear dry, insulating gloves and protective clothing
Wear approved head covering to protect head and neck
Wear approved welding jacket or long sleeves
Light from the arc can cause PERMANENT damage to eyes and skin
Welding and plasma cutting produce TOXIC fumes
Exposure can increase risk of cancer (larynx, lung), early-onset Parkinson's, heart disease, kidney damage, respiratory diseases
Use natural or forced air ventilation
Wear NIOSH-approved respirator
Keep head out of fumes
Work in confined areas only if well-ventilated or wearing air-supplied respirator
Clear flammable objects for radius of 35 feet (10 meters)
Keep ABC-type fire extinguisher nearby
Do not weld on materials with combustible coating or internal structure
After welding, examine thoroughly for evidence of fire
Do not apply heat to containers that held unknown or combustible substances

MIG.warning: Shielding gas can displace air and cause rapid loss of consciousness and death. Do not open gas without proper ventilation. Fix gas leaks immediately.
MIG.additional: Shielding gas without carbon dioxide (like pure Argon) can be even more hazardous because asphyxiation can start WITHOUT feeling shortness of breath.

MIG.warning: Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism. Close door before plugging in unless using Cold Wire Feed. Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun.
Keep welding tip clear of grounded objects whenever unit is plugged in and turned on. After welding, rest MIG Gun on electrically non-conductive, heat-proof surface.

Flux-Cored.warning: Same as MIG — keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism.
Flux-cored welds produce slag. Use chipping hammer carefully. Wear safety goggles during slag removal.

TIG.warning: 100% Argon can displace air and cause rapid loss of consciousness and death WITHOUT feeling shortness of breath. Do not open gas without proper ventilation. Fix gas leaks immediately.
TIG.critical_note: Pure Argon is MORE dangerous than CO2 mixes because you won't feel breathless before losing consciousness.

TIG.warning: When Right Knob is set to ON, the Welder is energized and Open Circuit Voltage (86 VDC) is present on the TIG Torch at all times — even when not welding.
TIG.rule: Always turn Right Knob to OFF before setting down torch.

TIG.warning: Do not weld without Grounding Clamp. Metal work bench MUST be grounded when TIG welding.
Some Electrodes have hazardous materials. Wear respirator and ANSI-approved safety goggles when grinding.
Only hold TIG Rod with electrically insulated welding glove.

Stick.warning: When Right Knob is set to ON, the Welder is energized and Open Circuit Voltage (86 VDC) is present on the Electrode Holder at all times.
Stick.rule: Always turn Right Knob to OFF before setting down Electrode Holder.

Stick.warning: Do not weld without Grounding Clamp.
Stick welds produce slag. Use chipping hammer carefully. Wear safety goggles during slag removal.

general_rules.extension_cords: Do NOT use an extension cord on this welder.
general_rules.vehicle_welding: When welding on a vehicle, disconnect battery from both positive and ground BEFORE welding. Prevents damage to electrical systems.
cylinder_safety: Do not weld on a pressurized or closed cylinder
Keep cylinders away from electrical circuits
Keep protective cap in place except when in use
Protect from heat, being struck, flames, sparks, arcs
